content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

All four amendments are technically valid. Two independently identify the same content-insensitive diff-fingerprint defect in iterative_minimal, and the other two correctly call out real retry/resume state loss and locale-sensitive non-git fallback behavior.

## Votes

- `fr-1-d4247189`: Accept
  `git_diff_fingerprint` is using `git status --porcelain` output as its fingerprint, and that output only records status classes plus paths. After a file is already dirty, further content edits still produce the same ` M path` or `?? path` line. Because `execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage` reduces progress detection to `diff_before != diff_after`, this can report `diff_changed = false` while bytes are still changing and can advance the stability counter to an early `stable` exit.
- `fr-1-65c2ecd2`: Accept
  The implementer loop state is not durably tracked. `execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage` initializes `iteration` and `stable_count` from local zeroes on every entry, while `ActiveRun` only persists QA/review/final-review counters and `resume_iteration_counters` restores only those. If `plan_and_implement` is retried or resumed mid-loop, the run can consume additional implementer rounds beyond `max_consecutive_implementer_rounds` and lose the prior stable streak, so the configured cap and stable-window semantics are not preserved across interruption boundaries.
- `fr-1-f9ace051`: Accept
  This is the same underlying defect as `fr-1-d4247189`, stated with stronger evidence. `git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all` is insensitive to byte-level changes inside an already-modified tracked file or an already-untracked file, so `diff_before != diff_after` can stay false even when the implementer changed workspace contents. The current regression coverage only exercises a clean-to-dirty transition, so it would not catch this false-stability case; a content-aware fingerprint and a test that mutates an already-dirty path are warranted.
- `fr-1-f79fad09`: Accept
  `is_non_git_workspace_error` is coupled to English stderr text, but `git_diff_fingerprint` runs git with the inherited locale. On systems where git localizes fatal messages, a non-repository workspace can fail the substring check and raise `StageCommitFailed` instead of falling back to `workspace_diff_fingerprint`. That is a real portability bug: non-git detection should not depend on localized human-readable stderr.
